description: §31 E-DG-001 NEGATIVE — the ACYCLIC equivalent of dg-001-cyclic-pos. Byte-identical source except `loadPet` does NOT call back to `loadOwner`, so the two db-escalated (§12.2 T1) server functions form a straight-line `awaits` chain `loadOwner -> loadPet` with a valid sequencing order. E-DG-001 stays silent. This is the cyclic-vs-acyclic contrast: server→server COMPOSITION is explicitly legal (§13.4) — only a CYCLE is rejected, so the whole E-DG- family must stay quiet here. Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED / W-SQL-ROW-UNTYPED ignored by superset.
rationale: §13.4: 'A server-escalated function MAY call another server-escalated function. Both execute in the same server handler context.' — a server→server awaits edge is legal by construction. §13.2 requires the compiler to 'Sequence dependent operations using `await`'; an acyclic awaits subgraph has a valid topological order, so the E-DG-001 rejection condition (§34: 'circular dependency in reactive graph') is not met and the compiler SHALL NOT reject the program.
